issues.body: The test still skips the real CircuitProver-to-capture path when nargo or bb is absent unless `SPARQ_ZK_REQUIRE_TOOLCHAIN` is set. No CI configuration in this diff sets that variable, so ordinary green test runs can continue without exercising bb's emitted proof and public-input artifacts. This is the same residual minor finding as round 2.
issues.fix_hint: Add or update a toolchain-equipped CI lane to run this test with `SPARQ_ZK_REQUIRE_TOOLCHAIN=1`, ensuring missing tools or incompatible emitted artifacts fail continuously.
issues.title: Required-toolchain mode remains unwired in CI

verdict.summary: Round 3 is materially unchanged from round 2. The plural `subProofs` schema remains consistent across the Rust serializer, generator, fixture, TypeScript consumer, and contract tests; the packaging rejects ragged public-input blobs and preserves proof bytes, and the ZK/composition claims remain explicitly research-grade and not externally audited. The prior minor coverage gap also remains: `SPARQ_ZK_REQUIRE_TOOLCHAIN` can make missing tools fail, but no CI configuration in this diff sets it, so the real native CircuitProver-to-capture path is still optional coverage. This does not invalidate the serialization change, but it has not improved since round 2.
